mnemonics: P.A.R.T.N.E.R.S. Betray: Players, Actions, Result, Terms, Nash Equilibrium, Rationality, Self-Interest. Betrayal leads to worse outcome.
keyPoints: A non-cooperative game where the Nash Equilibrium is Pareto inefficient.
The dominant strategy for both players is to defect (betray), even though mutual cooperation yields a better payoff.
It illustrates a conflict between individual rationality and collective good.
commonMistakes: Assuming communication or binding agreements can change the outcome in the classic one-shot version.
Confusing the Nash Equilibrium (both defect) with the optimal outcome (both cooperate).
